notes: Spec asserts state stays ready during two synchronous slash commands (/help, /status). Vibe never writes a slash command to messages.jsonl: _handle_command short-circuits before the input joins the agent-loop message list, and only that list is persisted (session_logger.persist_messages). The daemon tails messages.jsonl, so a slash command yields no ParsedEvent and no state transition — no local-command filter is needed (unlike claudecode). A warm-up turn goes FIRST to materialize the transcript (vibe writes messages.jsonl only after a real LLM turn) and birth an observable session; after warm_turn_end returns to ready, slash_window pins ready with duration_at_least_ms:4000 and invariants forbidding transitions to working/waiting — those are what would fire if /help or /status (incorrectly) drove a model call. Observations pin model=mistral-medium-3.5 (confirmed from live sidecars) + tokens_nonzero + agent; cost is NOT asserted (the vibe adapter emits no per-turn cost). Driver: wait_turn is a stubbed SEAM — record ports it (driver-gap) before recording; the slash step is fully supported.
source: .specs/agent-scenarios.md → Feature: Synchronous slash command (no LLM call)

text: Warm-up prompt drives ready -> working (real LLM turn; materializes messages.jsonl so the session births and later slash steps have a settled session to measure against)
Warm-up turn completes back to ready
Spec: state stays ready throughout /help and /status — neither synchronous slash command is written to messages.jsonl, so no transition fires
